name: Kimi K2.7 Code
archDisplay: 1T-parameter MoE, 32B active/token, 384 experts (8 routed + 1 shared), 61 layers (1 dense), MLA attention, native INT4 quantization support; coding-specialized post-train of the K2.6 base with forced/persistent thinking mode across multi-turn agent sessions
note: Coding/agent-specialized variant built on the Kimi K2.6 base weights, tuned for long-horizon software-engineering workflows and thousands of sequential tool calls, with ~30% fewer thinking tokens than K2.6 for equivalent tasks. A same-weights 'HighSpeed' serving SKU (kimi-k2.7-code-highspeed) also shipped, at roughly 2x the price ($1.90/M input cache-miss, $8.00/M output) for ~180-260 tok/s output — this is a serving/throughput tier, not a reasoning-effort tier, so it is not modeled as a separate entry here. All benchmark improvements Moonshot has published for this model to date are on Moonshot's own proprietary/in-house suites (Kimi Code Bench v2, Program Bench, MLS Bench Lite, Kimi Claw 24/7 Bench, MCP Atlas, MCP Mark Verified); no standard public-benchmark numbers (SWE-bench, LiveCodeBench, AIME, GPQA) have been disclosed for K2.7 Code specifically, and no independent third-party re-run results were found as of this research.

results.notes: Terminal-Bench v2.1, independently measured by Artificial Analysis as part of their Intelligence Index v4.1 methodology. Moonshot's own official model card (Hugging Face) discloses no standard public-benchmark numbers for K2.7 Code at all -- only proprietary in-house suites (Kimi Code Bench v2, Program Bench, MLS Bench Lite, Kimi Claw 24/7 Bench, MCP Atlas, MCP Mark Verified) -- so this independent re-run is the only real, sourced standard-benchmark data point available for this model. AA's independently-measured Kimi K2.6 score on the same eval (66%) closely corroborates K2.6's own vendor-reported Terminal-Bench 2.0 score of 66.7, supporting cross-comparability.
GPQA Diamond, independently measured by Artificial Analysis (Intelligence Index v4.1 component).

Humanity's Last Exam, independently measured by Artificial Analysis (Intelligence Index v4.1 component, no external tools). Note this is a different evaluation setting than the tool-augmented HLE figures recorded for sibling K2.5 (50.2) and K2.6 (54.0) entries.
IFBench, independently measured by Artificial Analysis.

τ²-Bench Telecom, independently measured by Artificial Analysis (multi-turn telecom customer-support agent eval, 114 tasks, pass@1 averaged over 3 repeats). Raw field 'tau2' = 0.900584795321637 in AA's underlying model-comparison dataset for slug 'kimi-k2-7-code', rendered on-page as 90.1%. Confirmed by cross-referencing both the rendered τ²-Bench Telecom leaderboard chart (artificialanalysis.ai/evaluations/tau2-bench, Kimi K2.7 Code position in ranked bar list) and the embedded JSON payload on that page. Not part of AA's Intelligence Index v4.1 composite (which uses the newer τ³-Banking instead), so this is a separate/legacy AA evaluation track. Moonshot's own official sources (Hugging Face model card, kimi.com resource page, platform.kimi.com quickstart) publish no tau-bench-family results at all for this model.

GDPval-AA v2 (agentic real-world knowledge-work tasks), independently measured by Artificial Analysis as an Intelligence Index v4.1 component. AA displays this as a normalized percentage via (Elo-500)/2000; raw Elo field 'gdpval_v2' = 1186.79 in AA's underlying dataset for slug 'kimi-k2-7-code' normalizes to 34.3%, matching the 34% shown on the rendered GDPval-AA v2 bar chart (Kimi K2.7 Code ranked 16th of 28 compared models). Cross-confirmed against both the rendered chart and the embedded JSON payload. [SCALE FIX 2026-07-17: corrected from a normalized percentage (34%) to this taxonomy's actual raw 0-2000-point Elo scale, using the exact raw value (1186.79) already disclosed earlier in this same note (AA's underlying JSON field 'gdpval_v2') rather than the formula-derived approximation.]

Official HF model card, Section 3 Evaluation Results > Agentic > MCP Atlas row. Raw README.md table: Kimi K2.6=69.4, Kimi K2.7 Code=76.0, GPT-5.5=79.4, Claude Opus 4.8=81.3 -- column headers confirm 76.0 is unambiguously this model's own score.

verificationNotes:
Confirmed real, released model. Architecture (1T/32B active/384 experts/61 layers/MLA/256K ctx/Modified MIT) verified on the official Hugging Face model card and matches K2.6's base architecture, consistent with K2.7 Code being a specialized post-train rather than a from-scratch model. Release date June 12, 2026 taken from MarkTechPost's same-day coverage and corroborated generally by the Moonshot forum announcement (which some mirrors date June 16) and kimi.com resource page (which one crawl dated June 25, likely a later content-refresh timestamp rather than the launch date) — flagging this date spread explicitly since sources disagree by up to ~2 weeks. Pricing $0.95/$4.00 per M tokens (standard variant) confirmed on the official platform.kimi.ai pricing page, which also lists a 'HighSpeed' SKU at double price. IMPORTANT CAVEAT: attempted to find independent/third-party benchmark verification (vals.ai, Artificial Analysis) but vals.ai's page returned all benchmarks as literal 0.0% scores for this model, which reads as a broken/unrendered scrape rather than real data — excluded as unusable. A VentureBeat piece ('Kimi K2.7-Code cuts thinking tokens 30% — but practitioners say the benchmarks don't check out') indicates practitioner skepticism about the vendor's proprietary benchmark claims, and multiple aggregators independently note that, as of release, Moonshot had published no results on standard public suites (SWE-bench, LiveCodeBench, GPQA) for this specific model — only on its own in-house benchmarks, all of which are logged below as self-reported with new (non-catalog) benchmark slugs since none map to an existing entry in the valid slug list.

2026-07-16: Researched the 7 core-benchmark gaps (mmlu, math, aime, humaneval, livecodebench, swe-bench, mmmu) and found no credible published score for any of them, so all seven are omitted rather than guessed. Checked official launch materials -- the Hugging Face model card (huggingface.co/moonshotai/Kimi-K2.7-Code), the Moonshot forum launch announcement (forum.moonshot.ai/t/here-comes-kimi-k2-7-code-better-coding-with-more-efficiency/441), the kimi.com resource page, and the platform.kimi.ai quickstart doc -- and all four consistently publish only Moonshot's proprietary in-house suites (Kimi Code Bench v2, Program Bench, MLS Bench Lite, Kimi Claw 24/7 Bench, MCP Atlas, MCP Mark Verified); none contain MMLU, MATH, AIME, HumanEval, LiveCodeBench, SWE-bench, or MMMU numbers for this model. Checked independent sources: Artificial Analysis's model page confirms their current Intelligence Index v4.1 composite for this model uses 9 evaluations (GDPval-AA v2, τ³-Banking, Terminal-Bench v2.1, SciCode, HLE, GPQA Diamond, CritPt, AA-Omniscience, AA-LCR) and explicitly shows no MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval/LiveCodeBench/SWE-bench/MMMU data for K2.7 Code; vals.ai (www.vals.ai/models/kimi_kimi-k2.7-code) still renders SWE-bench and LiveCodeBench rows for this model as literal 0.0% (a known broken/unrendered scrape, consistent with the prior finding already on file for this model, so unusable); llm-stats.com shows explicit "Score pending" placeholders for this model's benchmark rows; Epoch AI's benchmarking hub has no Kimi K2.7 Code entry at all. Several SEO/aggregator sites (automatio.ai, requesty.ai, benchlm.ai, kingy.ai, openlm.ai, flowtivity.ai, codersera.com) display specific-looking numbers (e.g. 78.2% SWE-bench, 68.5% LiveCodeBench, 87.2% MMLU, 91.5% AIME 2025, 94.2% HumanEval, 72.4% MMMU) but provide zero citations, and automatio.ai's own page simultaneously states elsewhere that "there are no independent third-party numbers for K2.7 on the standard public suites" -- a direct self-contradiction that, combined with the total absence of these figures from every official and independent primary source checked, marks them as fabricated/auto-generated content and disqualifies them as sources. This is consistent with the model's documented profile (see existing note/verificationNotes) as a specialized coding post-train whose vendor has to date only disclosed proprietary-suite results plus a handful of independently-run AA evaluations.

2026-07-17 (round-2 deep gap research, NEW source types only -- HELM, academic papers, GitHub, non-official leaderboards, non-English sources, sibling comparison tables): Re-chased mmlu, math, aime, humaneval using source types the 2026-07-16 pass had NOT checked (that pass covered official launch materials, Epoch AI, Vals.ai, LMArena, BenchLM). All 6 suggested new source types were checked; none produced a credible number, so all four gaps remain omitted.

1) Stanford HELM: fetched HELM's own config directly from GitHub (raw.githubusercontent.com/stanford-crfm/helm/main/src/helm/config/model_deployments.yaml and model_metadata.yaml) rather than relying on search snippets. Confirmed there is NO "kimi-k2.7" / "k2-7-code" entry anywhere in HELM's supported-model list -- only three older deployments exist (together/kimi-k2-instruct, together/kimi-k2-instruct-0905, together/kimi-k2-thinking), none of which is this model. Also loaded the live HELM Lite MMLU leaderboard page (crfm.stanford.edu) and confirmed it only contains legacy models (Claude 3.5, GPT-4o, Llama 3.x era) with no Kimi K2.7 Code row at all. HELM has never evaluated this specific model -- ruled out.

2) arXiv / Semantic Scholar: searched "Kimi K2.7" + MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval across arXiv and Semantic Scholar repeatedly. Only two Moonshot self-authored papers surface: Kimi K2 "Open Agentic Intelligence" (arXiv 2507.20534, July 2025, covers the original K2/K2-Instruct, not K2.7) and Kimi K2.5 "Visual Agentic Intelligence" (arXiv 2602.02276, Feb 2026, covers K2.5, not K2.7 Code). No independent paper introducing a new benchmark or method was found using Kimi K2.7 Code specifically as a reported baseline. Ruled out.

3) GitHub eval-harness logs: searched for lm-evaluation-harness results.json / benchmark-repo commits referencing "kimi-k2.7-code"; found none. The only GitHub-hosted material is Moonshot's own Kimi-K2 repo, which reproduces the same proprietary in-house suite numbers already on file (Kimi Code Bench v2, Program Bench, MLS Bench Lite, MCP Atlas, MCP Mark Verified, run under "OpenClaw harness" / Kimi Code CLI) -- no MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval anywhere in it. Ruled out.

4) Other leaderboards/aggregators not in the first pass's checklist: (a) LiveBench.ai -- loaded the live site directly in-browser (release 2026-06-25) and confirmed "Kimi K2.7 Code" IS present as a tested model: Overall 68.4, Reasoning 82.8, Coding 74.0, Agentic Coding 45.7, Mathematics 79.6, Data Analysis 62.7, Language 77.9, Instruction Following 56.3, $0.100/successful task. This is genuine independent, contamination-free third-party testing of this exact model -- but LiveBench's "Mathematics" and "Coding" columns are composite scores across LiveBench's own proprietary subtask mixes (deliberately NOT the static MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval sets, precisely because LiveBench's whole design goal is contamination-avoidance), so they cannot be honestly mapped onto the 'math'/'humaneval' slugs without conflating distinct benchmarks -- not used. (b) MathArena.ai -- an independent, rigorously-run AIME/competition-math leaderboard (4 runs/problem, cost-tracked) -- was checked directly in-browser; it does NOT list any Kimi model of any version (K2, K2.5, K2.6, K2.7 Code, K3) among its ~12 tested competitors at all, confirming no independent AIME run of this model exists there. (c) OpenRouter's real model page (openrouter.ai/moonshotai/kimi-k2.7-code, fetched directly, not an aggregator clone) shows pricing/throughput/Design-Arena creative-coding Elo data but no MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval scores. (d) PapersWithCode and Scale AI SEAL: no Kimi K2.7 Code entries found on either. Ruled out for all four benchmarks.

5) Non-English-language sources: checked Chinese Zhihu coverage of the launch (multiple articles from June 2026, including a "302.AI benchmark lab" hands-on test and a "first review" article) -- these report either Moonshot's own proprietary-suite deltas or a reviewer's private/manual question bank ("logic, mathematics, programming, multimodal" categories, not standardized MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval). Korean and Japanese searches surfaced only the same specific-looking numbers already flagged and dismissed as fabricated in the 2026-07-16 pass (87.2% MMLU, 94.2% HumanEval, 91.5% AIME 2025, 81.3% MATH, 71.4% MMLU-Pro) -- these figures are now also being recirculated on additional SEO clone sites (e.g. kimi-k2.org, requesty.ai) with zero citation or methodology, which if anything strengthens rather than weakens the conclusion that they are auto-generated/fabricated boilerplate rather than real measurements. Ruled out.

6) Sibling/competitor comparison tables: searched for any GPT-5.5, Claude Opus 4.8, or other 2026-vintage model's technical report/comparison table that lists Kimi K2.7 Code's MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval as a baseline reference point. Every comparison table found (across MarkTechPost, OpenRouter compare pages, dev.to, codersera, buildfastwithai, and Medium reviews) reuses only the same proprietary Kimi Code Bench v2 / Program Bench / MLS Bench Lite / Kimi Claw 24/7 / MCP Atlas / MCP Mark Verified rows already recorded on this model, several explicitly stating "no independent third-party results exist for K2.7 Code on ... MMLU-Pro" or "AIME" alongside those tables. Ruled out.

Net result: genuine, well-corroborated absence across HELM, academic literature, GitHub eval logs, independent leaderboards (LiveBench confirms the model IS independently tested, just not on these four specific classic benchmarks; MathArena confirms no Kimi model at all has been AIME-tested there), non-English community sources, and sibling comparison tables. All four gaps (mmlu, math, aime, humaneval) remain omitted rather than filled with an invented or low-confidence number.
